Name: records a buffering event and accumulates duration
Description: A start/end pair must produce exactly one buffering event and add the elapsed seconds to totalBufferingTime.

Name: does not count as a switch when the bitrate stays the same
Description: Receiving the same bitrate value twice must not increment the switch counter.

Name: all labels are non-empty strings
Description: Each label value must be a non-empty string suitable for display in a UI.

Name: returns null when only markPlaybackRequested has been called
Description: Without a first-frame mark there is no end time, so TTFF must remain null even after the request is marked.

Name: starts with zero bitrate switches and null lastBitrate
Description: No bitrate switches must be recorded until the first bitrateChange event fires.

Name: every row contains at least one video
Description: Rendering an empty row would produce a blank carousel — every row must have ≥ 1 video.

Name: accepts every known scenario id
Description: Every id in DEMO_SCENARIO_IDS must round-trip through parseDemoScenario unchanged.

Name: counts multiple switches correctly
Description: The switch counter must accurately reflect the number of quality-level transitions across the session.

Name: returns null when only markFirstFrameRendered has been called
Description: Without a playback-request mark there is no start time, so TTFF must remain null even after the first frame is marked.

Name: returns undefined for an empty string
Description: An empty string is not a valid video id and must return undefined.

Name: overrides a previously registered bridge
Description: A second call to registerQoeDemoBridge must replace the previous bridge so hot-reloads pick up the latest instance.

Name: returns null before markPlaybackRequested is called
Description: TTFF cannot be computed before the playback request has been marked — must return null.

Name: is findable by its id
Description: HERO_VIDEO must be reachable via findVideo so detail pages work when navigating from the hero banner.

Name: attaches the bridge to window.__QOE_DEMO__
Description: registerQoeDemoBridge must expose the bridge on window.__QOE_DEMO__ so Playwright tests can call getSnapshot() and control playback.

Name: finds every video present in ALL_VIDEOS by its id
Description: findVideo must successfully locate every video that exists in ALL_VIDEOS.

Name: is the first video in the "featured" row
Description: HERO_VIDEO powers the full-width hero banner; it must be the leading video in the "featured" row.

Name: tracks the last observed bitrate
Description: lastBitrate must always reflect the most recently recorded bitrate value.

Name: is case-sensitive — mixed-case ids fall back to baseline
Description: Scenario ids are case-sensitive; mixed-case variants must fall back to "baseline".

Name: all scenario values are valid DemoScenarioIds
Description: The scenario field is used for optional playback fault injection in CI tests.

Name: preserves the scenario value on the registered bridge
Description: The scenario property must survive registration so Playwright tests can read it from window.__QOE_DEMO__.scenario.

Name: markFirstFrameRendered is idempotent — subsequent calls are ignored
Description: Only the first call to markFirstFrameRendered captures the end mark; subsequent calls must not overwrite it.

Name: every row has a non-empty id and label
Description: Every catalog row must have a non-empty id (used as a React key) and a display label.

Name: increments the error count for each recorded error
Description: Each call to recordError must increment the error count by exactly one.

Name: all video IDs are unique
Description: Duplicate video IDs would cause React key collisions and routing bugs.

Name: all videos have a contentType
generates a unique sessionId for each instance (observable via lastBitrate independence)
Description: Each QoECollector instance must be fully independent — mutating one must not affect another.

Name: recordBufferingStart is idempotent while a buffering event is open
Description: A second recordBufferingStart while an event is already open must be ignored — the original start time is preserved.

Name: returns the elapsed ms between request and first frame
Description: TTFF = firstFrameMark − playbackRequestMark, measured in milliseconds via performance.now().

Name: returns the exact same object reference (no clone)
Description: findVideo should return the same reference stored in ALL_VIDEOS, not a copy, to enable identity comparisons.

Name: all videos have non-empty id, title, and description
Description: Every video must have the three fields required for display in a VideoTile component.

Name: counts a switch when the bitrate changes
Description: A transition from one bitrate to a different one must increment the switch counter.

Name: contains at least one row
Description: The catalog must expose at least one content row.

Name: all matchScore values are in the range 0–100
Description: matchScore drives the recommendation percentage badge (0–100); values outside that range would cause display bugs.

Name: movies, shows, and live tabs each have content
has no extra entries beyond the known scenario ids
Description: DEMO_SCENARIO_LABELS must not contain orphaned entries for unknown scenario ids.

Name: is the flat union of all CATALOG rows
Description: ALL_VIDEOS must equal the flat-mapped union of every catalog row — no extras, no omissions.

Name: accumulates multiple buffering events
Description: Each start/end pair must be tracked independently and their durations summed in totalBufferingTime.

Name: removes window.__QOE_DEMO__ when a bridge is registered
Description: unregisterQoeDemoBridge must delete window.__QOE_DEMO__ so tests that navigate away from the player cannot call a stale bridge.

Name: does not double-count if recordBufferingEnd is called twice
Description: A redundant second recordBufferingEnd must not add a phantom event.

Name: always includes "baseline" as the first entry
Description: "baseline" must be the first scenario — it is the reference stream used by other tests.

Name: defaults invalid values to baseline
Description: Null, empty string, and unrecognised values must all fall back to the "baseline" scenario.

Name: contains a "new-releases" row
Description: The new-releases row provides browse content for the home page.

Name: contains more than 5 videos
Description: The catalog must have a meaningful number of videos — a count ≤ 5 would indicate missing content.

Name: all year values are plausible (≥ 2000)
Description: Year values are displayed in the UI; obviously wrong years indicate a data entry error.

Name: does not count the first bitrate set as a switch
Description: The first bitrateChange event establishes a baseline; it must not be counted as a quality-level switch.

Name: contains exactly 5 scenario ids
Description: The catalogue must expose exactly 5 fault-injection scenario ids for CI tests.

Name: returns "baseline" for a string that is a prefix of a valid id
Description: Partial matches must not be accepted — only exact id strings are valid.

Name: all hlsUrl values are valid http(s) URLs
Description: HLS stream URLs must be fully-qualified http(s) addresses ending in .m3u8 so HLS.js can load them.

Name: contains a "featured" row
Description: The "featured" row drives the hero banner on the home page.

Name: ignores recordBufferingEnd when no start has been recorded
Description: Calling recordBufferingEnd without a prior recordBufferingStart must be a safe no-op.

Name: accumulates errors independently — each call adds exactly one
Description: Duplicate error codes are valid (the same error can recur); each call must add one entry regardless of code.

Name: is safe to call when no bridge is registered (idempotent)
Description: Calling unregisterQoeDemoBridge when no bridge is registered must not throw — it should be a safe no-op.

Name: markPlaybackRequested is idempotent — subsequent calls are ignored
Description: Only the first call to markPlaybackRequested sets the start mark; subsequent calls must be silently ignored so TTFF is not reset mid-session.

Name: starts with zero errors
Description: Error count must be zero before any playback errors are recorded.

Name: has a label entry for every known scenario id
Description: Every scenario id must have a corresponding human-readable label.

Name: starts with zero buffering count and time
Description: A freshly constructed QoECollector must report zero buffering events and zero accumulated buffering time.

Name: has a baseline scenario
Description: The hero video should use the baseline scenario for normal playback.

Name: returns undefined for an unknown id
Description: findVideo must return undefined when no video matches the given id.

Name: resolves without throwing
Description: In silent mode (used in CI without a backend) sendMetrics must return immediately without making any HTTP call or throwing.
